sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after MOUSE click on magenta
seg_before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
seg_after = current_frame.segmentation

print("=== BEFORE (magenta/charcoal) ===")
for n in seg_before['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'M'):
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\n=== AFTER (magenta/charcoal) ===")
for n in seg_after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'M'):
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check ALL nodes for any changes
print("\n=== ALL AFTER ===")
for n in seg_after['nodes']:
    print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

4. The yellow containers are at the bottom
5. The charcoal/magenta blocks at top don't seem interactive

Looking at the left yellow container: cols 16-27 (center at col 21.5)
The blue rectangle is 20 cols wide. To center it above the left container, I'd need the blue rect at cols 12-31 (center at col 21.5). That's exactly where it is now!
Wait, the blue rectangle is currently at cols 12-31. The left yellow container spans cols 16-27. The blue rect's center is at col 21.5, and the left container's center is also at col 21.5. So they're already horizontally aligned!
But the blue rectangle can't move down to row 52 where the container is. Maybe I need to move it to a specific vertical position and then press SPACE?
Or maybe the game is about getting the blue rectangle to overlap with the yellow containers in some way. But the blue rectangle can't go below row 47.

- But the blue rect can only move within a limited vertical range.
